name: categorize-food-definitions
description: Find uncategorized food definition names and assign them to an existing or new category in definitions-food-categories.json. Use when auditing food categories, fixing Uncategorized counts, or when the user asks to categorize foods / update the category map.
purpose: Look for any food names that match no category pattern, decide whether each belongs in an existing category (extend patterns) or needs a new category, then update definitions-food-categories.json and re-check until the uncategorized list is empty or only true leftovers remain.
onInvoke: Explain the purpose: find foods with no category match, place them into an existing category or propose a new one, and update definitions-food-categories.json.
Confirm which food list to audit (see foodSources). If the user already named a file or pasted names, use that. Otherwise ask.
Run the list-uncategorized helper (or equivalent) against that food list + definitions-food-categories.json.
If uncategorized count is 0, report that and stop unless the user wants a category-structure review.

foodSources.prompt: Which food list should I check for uncategorized names?

options.label: A pasted list of food names only

foodSources.defaultHint: Prefer samples/definitions-food.json for repo work; use an export when matching the user's live browser list.

rules: id — stable camelCase or short slug (e.g. cereal, bread); used as keywordsCategoryFilter value
label — human label shown in the Categories modal / chip
patterns — case-insensitive regex strings; first matching category in array order wins
Foods matching no pattern are Uncategorized

matchingRules: Prefer anchored name prefixes (^Name) over loose substring matches.
Cover known naming variants in one category (e.g. Cereal - … and Cereals Cheerios → ^Cereals?\b).
Do not steal foods from another category: check first-match order after edits.

Keep patterns specific enough that unrelated foods stay out (e.g. avoid bare ^Rice if it would catch Cereal - … Rice Krispies when cereal is later in the list — cereal should stay above grains, or patterns must stay precise).
New category id must be unique; label should fit the modal list style (short noun phrase).

existingCategory: Name shares a clear family with foods already counted in a category (same prefix, brand family, or food type).
A small pattern addition (prefix / alias) would cover this food and likely siblings.
Action: add or broaden a pattern on that category; do not duplicate the food into multiple categories.
newCategory: Several uncategorized foods form a coherent group with no fitting existing label.
Or one food type would force an existing category to become too broad / misleading.
Action: append a new { id, label, patterns } entry; place it so first-match order does not steal from earlier categories.
leaveUncategorized: One-off or ambiguous name with no stable pattern.
Or user asks not to invent a category yet.
Action: list it under leftovers; do not force a bad match.

workflow: Load definitions-food-categories.json and the selected food list.
Run: node scripts/list-uncategorized-foods.js --json <foodsPath>   (omit path to use samples/definitions-food.json).
Group uncategorized names by likely family (shared prefix, food type, brand).
For each group, decide: existing category | new category | leave.
Propose edits before writing when the user has not asked to apply automatically: show food → proposed category id/label → pattern change.
Apply approved edits to definitions-food-categories.json only (not the food JSON names, unless the user explicitly asks to rename foods).
Re-run the list script. Repeat until uncategorized is empty or only agreed leftovers remain.
If a new category was added, mention it so AGENTS docs stay accurate only if the user asks for doc updates — category map file is the source of truth for the app.

sections: Source food file + uncategorized count before/after
Assigned to existing categories (food → category id + pattern added)
New categories created (id, label, patterns, member foods)

notes: Category rules live only in definitions-food-categories.json; they are not stored on food objects.
Browser localStorage food lists can use older names than samples/definitions-food.json — always audit the list the user cares about.
Editing a pattern can move many foods at once; after edits, verify category counts and that previously categorized foods did not jump categories unexpectedly.
Do not delete categories or rewrite unrelated patterns unless the user asks.
Helper script and app matching both use first match + case-insensitive RegExp; keep that contract.
